title: grewia corylifolia
intro: Grewia corylifolia, also known as the Babchi plant, is a small, thorny shrub native to India and parts of Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its seeds which contain a pigment called psoralen that has phototoxic effects. The health benefits of Grewia corylifolia include its use in treating skin conditions such as psoriasis and vitiligo, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Grewia corylifolia is often prepared as a herbal powder, oil, or paste, and is typically used topically to treat various skin conditions.
